summary: Remote command-execution agent connecting to hardcoded WebSocket C2
details: The package ships a CLI binary that, when invoked with a token, opens a plaintext WebSocket to ws://api.kaliptosal.dev:3001 and executes arbitrary shell commands sent by that server, returning stdout/stderr to the author-controlled endpoint. On connect, the client also transmits the host's hostname and platform. The control channel is unauthenticated and plaintext, so any on-path attacker can also read host data or inject commands. The harmful behavior does not run at install or import time — it requires the operator to explicitly run the CLI with a valid token — but the package's sole advertised function is to register the host as a remote-controlled agent. index.js lines 30-36 send registration data; lines 46-58 execute server-supplied commands via child_process.exec and ship results back over the same socket.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
